Politics.headline: Labour blame game begins as Plaid Cymru poised to lead Wales
Politics.detail: A political landscape once dominated by Labour has been transformed overnight, with Plaid Cymru emerging as the biggest party and Reform UK making major gains.

Politics.headline: Virginia's high court strikes down voter-passed House map favoring Democrats
Politics.detail: The Virginia Supreme Court has struck down a voter-approved House map that favored Democrats, leading to potential redistricting.

Politics.headline: Peter Magyar sworn in as Hungary's PM, ending Orban's 16 years in power
Politics.detail: Peter Magyar has been sworn in as the new Prime Minister of Hungary, marking the end of Viktor Orban's 16-year tenure.

Politics.headline: Laura Fernandez sworn in as Costa Rica's new president
Politics.detail: Laura Fernandez has been sworn in as the new president of Costa Rica.

Politics.headline: How MAGA Sheriff Chad Bianco is shaking up the 2026 California gubernatorial primary
Politics.detail: Riverside Sheriff Chad Bianco's says he will restore law and order to California if he is elected governor, a claim critics dispute.

Finance & Economy.headline: Trump Media and Technology Group lost $406m in first three months of 2026
Finance & Economy.detail: Donald Trump's media company, Trump Media and Technology Group, reported a significant loss of $406 million in the first quarter of 2026, raising concerns about its financial stability.

Finance & Economy.headline: US added 115,000 jobs in April in surprise gain amid Iran war uncertainty
Finance & Economy.detail: Despite ongoing tensions related to the Iran war, the U.S. economy added a surprisingly strong 115,000 jobs in April, signaling resilience in the labor market.

Finance & Economy.headline: Saudi Arabia posts $33.5bn budget deficit amid drop in oil sales
Finance & Economy.detail: The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia announced a sharp rise in its budget deficit, reaching $33.5 billion, due to a drop in oil sales amid the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

Finance & Economy.headline: India absorbs global oil shock; OMC under-recoveries swell to Rs 30,000 crore monthly
Finance & Economy.detail: Despite the global oil shock, India has managed to absorb the impact, though oil marketing companies (OMC) are facing under-recoveries that have surged to Rs 30,000 crore monthly.

Finance & Economy.headline: Pakistan secures $1.32 billion IMF funding amid push for reforms, economic stability.
Finance & Economy.detail: Pakistan has secured $1.32 billion in funding from the IMF as it continues to pursue economic reforms and stability.

Technology.headline: Canvas Restored After Hack, Breach Traced to 'Free-For-Teacher' Accounts
Technology.detail: The online learning platform Canvas, used by 30 million students, was hacked in a massive cyberattack, but service has since been restored; the breach was traced to 'Free-For-Teacher' accounts.

Technology.headline: This Startup Wants to Put a Mini Data Center in Your Backyard
Technology.detail: Span claims consumers can slash their energy and internet bills by installing a miniature 'distributed data center' in their own homes.

Technology.headline: AI cuts wildlife tracking time from months to days
Technology.detail: AI is being used to significantly reduce the time it takes to track wildlife, from months to days.

Technology.headline: Cloudflare cuts 1,100 jobs despite record revenue, says AI made roles obsolete
Technology.detail: Cloudflare has cut 1,100 jobs despite reporting record revenue, citing that AI has made some roles obsolete.

Technology.headline: Samsung watches can predict if you're about to faint - but there are big caveats
Technology.detail: Samsung watches now have the capability to predict if the wearer is about to faint, though there are some limitations.

Science.headline: Physicists Discover Quantum Particles That Break the Rules of Reality
Science.detail: Physicists may have just discovered a hidden side of the quantum world, potentially cracking open new possibilities in quantum physics. These newly discovered particles exhibit behaviors that defy conventional understanding of how the quantum world operates.

Science.headline: New Chemical Kills 95% of Termites Without Harming Humans
Science.detail: Scientists may have found a smarter, safer way to eliminate termites hiding inside homes using a chemical called bistrifluron. This chemical prevents drywood termites from forming new exoskeletons during molting, effectively killing entire colonies from within.

Science.headline: Scientists Create “Quantum Sound” Device That Works Near Absolute Zero
Science.detail: A new device has been created that generates 'quantum sound' and operates near absolute zero, potentially enabling advances in high-speed communication, sensing tools and medical materials. The device may also support advances in biological materials.

Science.headline: New AI Blood Test Detects Silent Liver Disease Before Symptoms Appear
Science.detail: An AI-powered blood test has been developed that analyzes DNA fragments to detect silent liver disease long before symptoms appear. This early detection could significantly improve patient outcomes by enabling timely intervention.

Science.headline: AI-powered electrocardiogram detects early signs of heart failure
Science.detail: An artificial intelligence (AI) algorithm can accurately screen patients for a key precursor of heart failure by interpreting relatively inexpensive electrocardiograms (ECGs). This was demonstrated in a study conducted in Kenya, suggesting potential for widespread use in areas with limited resources.

Health.headline: WHO chief reassures Tenerife residents as hantavirus-hit cruise ship nears Canary Islands
Health.detail: WHO Director General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus reassured residents that the public health risk remained low as the cruise ship MV Hondius approached the Canary Islands following a hantavirus outbreak onboard; the vessel, carrying 147 people, is expected to arrive between 4 a.m. and 6 a.m. local time Sunday.

Health.headline: Sudan Aviation Center Issues Hantavirus Warning
Health.detail: The Sudanese Centre for Civil Aviation Studies and Research warned of the risks of Hantavirus and called on aviation authorities to implement urgent health precautions at airports as international travel resumes. This follows a recent WHO report regarding a deadly outbreak of the Andes strain of Hantavirus linked to a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean.

Health.headline: Americans from hantavirus-hit cruise ship to quarantine in Nebraska
Health.detail: U.S. passengers on a cruise struck by hantavirus will soon head to a federal quarantine facility in Nebraska. U.S. officials are monitoring symptoms of seven returning passengers in at least five states: Arizona, California, Georgia, Texas and Virginia.

Health.headline: WHO presents major primary healthcare reform report
Health.detail: The World Health Organization (WHO) has formally handed over a comprehensive assessment report on Sri Lanka's health system, outlining key recommendations to strengthen primary healthcare and advance the country towards universal health coverage. The report highlights the need to further strengthen primary healthcare as the foundation of Sri Lanka's health system, identifying existing gaps, challenges and reform opportunities.

Health.headline: Iran's supreme leader in 'full health' after injuries in US-Israeli attack during war: Report
Health.detail: Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei is in “full health” after sustaining injuries during a joint US-Israeli attack on the office compound of Iran's leadership on the first day of the war earlier this year, a senior Iranian official reported. He suffered injuries to his kneecap and lower back.

Sports.headline: Wembanyama matches Kareem, Hakeem and Shaq with his superb stat line for Spurs in Game 3 win over Wolves
Sports.detail: Victor Wembanyama delivered another stellar performance, leading the Spurs to a 115-108 victory over the Timberwolves and a 2-1 series lead. Wembanyama's impressive stats put him in the company of NBA legends.

Sports.headline: Jalen Brunson scores 33 points and helps Knicks take 3-0 lead over 76ers with 108-94 Game 3 win
Sports.detail: Jalen Brunson's 33-point performance propelled the New York Knicks to a 108-94 victory over the Philadelphia 76ers, giving them a commanding 3-0 lead in the series. The Knicks are now one win away from advancing in the playoffs.

Sports.headline: Jets, running back Breece Hall agree to a 3-year, $45.75 million contract extension, AP source says
Sports.detail: Breece Hall and the New York Jets have agreed to a 3-year, $45.75 million contract extension, according to an AP source. This solidifies Hall's position as a key player for the Jets.

Sports.headline: Allen, Kraus lead Sea Bears to comeback win over Stingers in CEBL season opener
Sports.detail: Hometown player Mason Kraus hit two free throws to give the Winnipeg Sea Bears a 77-75 victory over the Edmonton Stingers in the Canadian Elite Basketball League's opening game. The Sea Bears secured a comeback win in front of their home crowd.

Sports.headline: Daniel Dubois stops Fabio Wardley to become WBO heavyweight champion
Sports.detail: Daniel Dubois defeated Fabio Wardley to become the WBO heavyweight champion. The fight was stopped in the 11th round.

Entertainment.headline: 'Patriot' box office collection day 10: Mammootty-Mohanlal film holds steady; Earns Rs 90 lakh
Entertainment.detail: The Malayalam film 'Patriot', directed by Mahesh Narayanan and starring Mammootty and Mohanlal, earned Rs 90 lakh in India on Day 10, showing stable performance despite mixed reviews. The movie, which brought together the two biggest icons of Malayalam cinema after a long gap, has seen its momentum slow significantly compared to its opening week.

Entertainment.headline: Katy Perry will perform at SoFi Stadium before first World Cup game in US
Entertainment.detail: Katy Perry will perform at SoFi Stadium before the first World Cup game in the United States, as announced by FIFA. The entertainment lineup for the initial matches in the three host nations (United States, Mexico, and Canada) has been revealed.

Entertainment.headline: Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni have ended their 'It Ends With Us' dispute in a settlement
Entertainment.detail: Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni have settled their legal dispute over the production of their 2024 film 'It Ends With Us'. The details of the settlement were not disclosed, but it resolves the acrimonious legal battle between the parties.

Entertainment.headline: Olivia Rodrigo's 'Unraveled' Tour 2026: Ticket Prices, Presale Date & More
Entertainment.detail: Olivia Rodrigo has announced her 'Unraveled Tour' for 2026, and fans are eagerly awaiting details on ticket prices and presale dates. Information regarding tickets and dates for the tour is now available.

Entertainment.headline: Shakira teases new song for the FIFA World Cup 2026 with Afrobeats star Burna Boy called 'Dai Dai'
Entertainment.detail: Shakira is making a comeback with a new song titled 'Dai Dai' featuring Afrobeats star Burna Boy, which is teased for the FIFA World Cup 2026. This announcement suggests Shakira's continued involvement in major international events.

Crime & Justice.headline: Special Forces vet accused of attacking wife found dead, ending Tennessee manhunt
Crime & Justice.detail: The retired Army Special Forces Veteran accused of trying to kill his wife is dead after the incident sparked a massive multi-agency manhunt, according to an official.

Crime & Justice.headline: Killer in Colorado professor slaying makes disturbing comment after being dragged to court
Crime & Justice.detail: A Colorado killer who mocked the court, skipped his own sentencing and forced a judge to order that he be brought in "by any means necessary" is now facing the rest of his life behind bars.

Crime & Justice.headline: Pentagon says 1 survivor after latest strike on alleged drug boat kills 2
Crime & Justice.detail: The U.S. military's latest strike on an alleged drug-trafficking boat in the eastern Pacific Ocean killed two men Friday while leaving one survivor.

Crime & Justice.headline: Make D.C. Safe and Beautiful Task Force Arrests Juvenile Murder Suspect
Crime & Justice.detail: On May 5, 2026, members of the United States Marshals Service (USMS) Capital Area Regional Fugitive Task Force (CARFTF), as part of the Make D.C. Safe and Beautiful operation, arrested a juvenile suspect wanted for a murder that took place on April 25, 2026.

Crime & Justice.headline: U.S. Marshals Capture Suspect Wanted for Attempted Murder in Delaware
Crime & Justice.detail: Members of the U.S. Marshals Service Albany Division of the New York/New Jersey Regional Fugitive Task Force (NY/NJ RFTF) arrested Emanuel Greaves who was wanted by the New Castle County, Delaware Police Department on an arrest warrant for attempted murder in the first degree (2 counts) and possession of a firearm.

Education.headline: UAE to announce in-person or remote classes on May 10 after safety review
Education.detail: The Ministry of Education and Dubai's Knowledge and Human Development Authority will announce on the evening of Sunday, May 10, whether schools will continue with distance learning or return to in-person classes, following an assessment of the current situation in coordination with relevant authorities. The move is aimed at ensuring the safety of students and staff while maintaining the continuity of the education process.

Education.headline: Young children need pencils, not phones
Education.detail: Experts are raising concerns about the increasing amount of time young children spend on mobile phones, which is linked to issues such as short attention spans and antisocial behavior. Some countries, including Malaysia, have national bans on mobile phones in schools to improve concentration and strengthen social skills.

Education.headline: 'Teachers still matter'
Education.detail: A community service programme in Penang, jointly organised by USM and the Buddhist Tzu-Chi Merits Society Malaysia, pairs students with near-age tutors for one-on-one guidance, providing support tailored to each learner's level. This support provides encouragement and creates a lasting impact on the lives of underprivileged students.

Education.headline: Academicians wanted loosening of government control of universities. Yet the prime minister's grip has only tightened
Education.detail: Education experts and academics are criticizing a new provision that allows the prime minister to directly appoint student and teacher representatives. They argue that universities should belong to professors, students, and employees, not be controlled by the prime minister.

Education.headline: Sabah Education Minister defends 'no dilapidated schools' remark after backlash
Education.detail: The Sabah Education Minister is defending their remark about there being no dilapidated schools.

Lifestyle.headline: Emotional burden of infertility can undermine lifestyle choices that may help women conceive
Lifestyle.detail: A global conference in Beijing heard that the emotional stress of infertility actively undermines a woman's capacity to make sustained lifestyle changes, like weight loss and physical activity, that could improve their chances of becoming pregnant. Up to 60% of women struggling with infertility experience mental health challenges, yet only about 20% seek professional help.

Lifestyle.headline: Met Gala 2026 live: stars walk the carpet on fashion's biggest night – as it happened
Lifestyle.detail: The Met Gala, fashion's biggest night, took place, showcasing stars walking the red carpet in artistic and creative outfits. The event highlighted fashion as art, with attendees interpreting the theme through various designs and styles.

Lifestyle.headline: US coordinating flight for Americans aboard hantavirus-stricken cruise ship
Lifestyle.detail: After a hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship, the US is coordinating a flight for American passengers. This comes amidst concerns and discussions about viral outbreaks on cruise ships.

Lifestyle.headline: Delta is cutting snacks and drinks on short flights.
Lifestyle.detail: Delta Airlines is reducing the availability of snacks and drinks on shorter flights in an effort to create a more consistent customer experience. The airline is eliminating its express service, which includes these complimentary items.

Lifestyle.headline: Gloucestershire May half term guide.
Lifestyle.detail: A guide to events in Gloucestershire including the RHS Malvern Spring Festival, Gozzle trail at Slimbridge Wetland Centre, LEGO Brick History at Berkeley Castle, Double Double at Barn Theatre Cirencester, and trails at Westonbirt Arboretum. These events provide various options for family activities during the May half-term.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL — Things have to change
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The editorial discusses the enforcement of the Safer Cities Initiative in Cebu City, Philippines, which prohibits drinking in public, being shirtless in public places, singing karaoke late at night, and enforces the curfew for minors.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Protecting Boracay
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial discusses San Miguel Holdings Corp.'s commitment to address local government concerns about the Boracay bridge project.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: Beijing is afraid of a strong Japan
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial discusses Japan's updated “Free and Open Indo-Pacific” (FOIP) strategy and its implications for regional security, particularly in relation to Taiwan and China.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: World Press Freedom Day 2026
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This article emphasizes the importance of protecting the right to freedom of expression and the dangers faced by journalists worldwide, particularly in conflict zones.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Corruption with no mastermind
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This opinion piece discusses a Senate report on flood control investigations in the Philippines, highlighting corruption that occurred across various regions without central coordination, and the lack of a strong commitment to enforce the law and protect the system of governance from being corrupted.

World.headline: Three hikers dead after Indonesia's Mount Dukono volcano erupts
World.detail: Three hikers in an off-limits area died in an eruption of Indonesia's Mount Dukono volcano, according to police reports. A search operation was launched after the eruption on the remote island.

World.headline: What we know about hantavirus cases tied to deadly cruise ship outbreak
World.detail: Health officials have identified at least nine confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus tied to an outbreak on the M/V Hondius cruise ship. A special biocontainment unit in Nebraska is preparing to host at least 17 people for mandatory isolation.

World.headline: Israeli attacks across Lebanon kill at least 23
World.detail: Israeli attacks across Lebanon have resulted in the death of at least 23 people. This violence occurs amidst ongoing tensions and ceasefire violations in the region.

World.headline: US military kills two people in new eastern Pacific Ocean attack
World.detail: The U.S. military conducted a strike on an alleged drug-trafficking boat in the eastern Pacific Ocean, resulting in the death of two individuals. The Pentagon reports there was one survivor from the attack.

World.headline: Péter Magyar sworn in as Hungary's prime minister ending 16-year Orbán era
World.detail: Péter Magyar has been sworn in as the new Prime Minister of Hungary, marking the end of Viktor Orbán's 16-year tenure. This transition signifies a significant shift in Hungarian politics.

Zimbabwe.headline: Minister urges youths to use skills for nation building . . .
Zimbabwe.detail: The Minister has urged youths to use their skills for nation-building as 788 graduate at IYF Academy.

Zimbabwe.headline: President delivers last batch of vehicles for youths
Zimbabwe.detail: The President has delivered the last batch of vehicles for youths.

Zimbabwe.headline: Hero's son skips court, warrant issued
Zimbabwe.detail: A warrant has been issued after a hero's son skipped court.

Zimbabwe.headline: Major gas drilling campaign ignites Zimbabwe energy hopes
Zimbabwe.detail: A major gas drilling campaign has ignited Zimbabwe's energy hopes.

Zimbabwe.headline: Tycoon's daughter makes nurses dance for cash, pledges 3,500 hampers per month to Parirenyatwa health workers
Zimbabwe.detail: The tycoon's daughter made nurses dance for cash and pledged 3,500 hampers per month to Parirenyatwa health workers.
